upgrade_contract.major_version_boundary_rule: Breaking removals and incompatible semantic changes require a major version boundary.
upgrade_contract.deprecation_window_policy: Machine-consumable surfaces remain available for at least one full minor release after deprecation is published in a manifest.
upgrade_contract.behavior_change_policy: Behavior changes that tighten validation or deny previously accepted input must be classified in the manifest.

summary: Theorem 4.0.1 is the first public 4.x machine-contract release, carrying release integrity and explicit substrate trust for BIOS-mode cloud VMs, plus the E4 qualification fix that allowed the rig-built release to validate cleanly on the production build path.

new.summary: Adds theorem-native declaration of tiered boot integrity so BIOS-mode cloud VMs can report explicit trust degradation instead of silently omitting boot-chain state.
Adds machine-readable release integrity evidence with SBOM, provenance, rollback authorization, revocation, and fail-closed mirror-expiry metadata.

changed.summary: Governed release publication now carries release_integrity metadata in the manifest and is expected to ship an integrity evidence asset for 4.x consumers.

capability_negotiation.surface: releases/feed.json plus releases/v0.4.1/manifest.json, with live runtime discovery at GET /capabilities and GET /capability-negotiation
capability_negotiation.stability_rule: Capability ids published in a 4.x manifest are stable within the 4.x line once released.
consumer_rules: Gate on compatibility_classification before enabling a new capability id.
Treat manifest capability ids and release_integrity metadata as authoritative rather than inferring behavior from prose notes or semver alone.
Use releases/feed.json, releases/v0.4.1/manifest.json, and releases/v0.4.1/integrity.json as the release-time capability source of record for public 4.x releases.
Use GET /capabilities and GET /capability-negotiation to confirm the live runtime discovery surface for a running 4.x node.

semantics: entity_id names the acting or queried entity on public request surfaces.
principal_id names the entity holding authority on authorization surfaces.
granted_by_id names the grantor and must not be interpreted as the principal.
GET /authorizations returns authorizations held by the queried principal entity, not authorizations granted by it.

stable_error_taxonomy.scope: Stable for the published 4.0 discovery, organization, lifecycle, release-verification, and governed-release routes in openapi.yaml.
stable_error_taxonomy.legacy_behavior: Legacy pre-4.0 routes may still return text/plain bodies and are outside this stable taxonomy.

declare_intent.enforcement_rule: Acts must satisfy E4: declared_at plus minimum_intent_execution_interval_ms must be less than or equal to initiated_at.

required_operator_actions: Consume releases/feed.json, releases/v0.4.1/manifest.json, and releases/v0.4.1/integrity.json before mirroring or admitting a 4.x release.
Treat substrate_trust_declaration.boot_integrity_chain as the canonical runtime trust-tier signal for deployed machines.
Use the theorem-cloud flake target and theorem.substrateTier=2 when publishing BIOS-mode cloud deployments that rely on Nix store verification instead of UEFI Secure Boot.

release_integrity.downgrade_prevention_rule: Once a consumer admits the 4.x public feed, it must not mirror or promote a release older than release_integrity.minimum_version without an explicit governed rollback decision.

rollback_authorization.rule: Rollback to an older 4.x artifact requires explicit governed approval before repointing a machine or mirror.
